title: Comparative effectiveness and safety of inhaled corticosteroid plus long-acting β
abstract: This study aimed to evaluate the effectiveness and safety of fixed-dose combination (FDC) inhaled corticosteroids/long-acting β2-agonists (ICS/LABA) in bronchiectasis. A retrospective cohort study analyzed electronic medical records of bronchiectasis patients initiating ICS/LABA FDC or LAMA between 2007 and 2021. All bronchiectasis diagnoses were made by radiologists using high-resolution computed tomography. Of the 1,736 patients, 1,281 took ICS/LABA FDC and 455 LAMA. Among the 694 propensity score matched patients, ICS/LABA FDC had comparable outcomes to LAMA, with HRs of 1.22 (95% CI 0.81-1.83) for hospitalized respiratory infection, 1.06 (95% CI 0.84-1.33) for acute exacerbation, and 1.06 (95% CI 0.66-1.02) for all-cause hospitalization. Beclomethasone/formoterol (BEC/FOR) or budesonide/formoterol (BUD/FOR) led to a lower risk of acute exacerbation compared to fluticasone/salmeterol (FLU/SAL) (BEC/FOR HR 0.59, 95% CI 0.43-0.81; BUD/FOR HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.50-0.93). BEC/FOR resulted in lower risks of hospitalized respiratory infection (HR 0.48, 95% 0.26-0.86) and all-cause hospitalization (HR 0.55, 95% 0.37-0.80) compared to FLU/SAL. Our findings provide important evidence on the effectiveness and safety of ICS/LABA FDC compared with LAMA for bronchiectasis. BEC/FOR and BUD/FOR were associated with better outcomes than FLU/SAL.

screening.reason: The study evaluates the effectiveness and safety of inhaled corticosteroids (ICS) for bronchiectasis, which is directly relevant to the CLEF TAR topic.

overall_concept_summary: The study compares fixed-dose combination inhaled corticosteroids/long-acting β2-agonists (ICS/LABA) with long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMA) for bronchiectasis.
detailed_summary: This retrospective cohort study analyzed the effectiveness and safety of ICS/LABA FDC compared to LAMA in bronchiectasis patients. The study found comparable outcomes between the two groups, but fixed-dose combinations like beclomethasone/formoterol (BEC/FOR) or budesonide/formoterol (BUD/FOR) showed better results than fluticasone/salmeterol (FLU/SAL).
methods_summary: A retrospective cohort study analyzed electronic medical records of bronchiectasis patients initiating ICS/LABA FDC or LAMA between 2007 and 2021.
results_summary: ICS/LABA FDC had comparable outcomes to LAMA, with HRs for hospitalized respiratory infection, acute exacerbation, and all-cause hospitalization. BEC/FOR and BUD/FOR led to lower risks of acute exacerbation, hospitalized respiratory infection, and all-cause hospitalization compared to FLU/SAL.
conclusion_summary: The study provides evidence on the effectiveness and safety of ICS/LABA FDC for bronchiectasis, with some fixed-dose combinations showing better outcomes than others.
relevance_to_review: Highly relevant as it directly addresses inhaled corticosteroids for bronchiectasis.
key_takeaways: ICS/LABA FDC had comparable outcomes to LAMA
BEC/FOR and BUD/FOR showed better results than FLU/SAL
objective: To evaluate the effectiveness and safety of ICS/LABA FDC in bronchiectasis.
population_or_sample: 1,736 bronchiectasis patients initiating ICS/LABA FDC or LAMA between 2007 and 2021
main_results: HRs for hospitalized respiratory infection, acute exacerbation, and all-cause hospitalization were comparable between ICS/LABA FDC and LAMA.
BEC/FOR and BUD/FOR had lower risks of acute exacerbation, hospitalized respiratory infection, and all-cause hospitalization compared to FLU/SAL.
conclusions: ICS/LABA FDC is effective and safe for bronchiectasis
Some fixed-dose combinations (BEC/FOR, BUD/FOR) show better outcomes than others (FLU/SAL)
evidence_quotes_or_locations: Among the 694 propensity score matched patients, ICS/LABA FDC had comparable outcomes to LAMA, with HRs of 1.22 (95% CI 0.81-1.83) for hospitalized respiratory infection, 1.06 (95% CI 0.84-1.33) for acute exacerbation, and 1.06 (95% CI 0.66-1.02) for all-cause hospitalization.
